extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon attacked 1300m from the finish on the cobbled finale in Osimo, dropped DUMOULIN Tom on the flattening section to win.
tactical_patterns: YATES Simon attacked where cobbles transitioned to flatter gradient, exploiting power differential not just climbing ability with 1300m remaining.
Quick-Step forced early pace on cobbled approach at 4.7km, releasing rivals off the leash before final surge.
DUMOULIN Tom closed gap on steep cobbled section but cracked precisely where road flattened, revealing sustained power weakness.
KREUZIGER Roman paced for YATES early on climb, teammates holding position rather than defending demonstrates calculated team confidence.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Second stage win demonstrates repeatable ability to time attacks on unconventional finales, barely mouth-breathing at winning effort
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: Closed gap on steep cobbles but lost ground when gradient eased, suggesting power sustainability issues over climbing punch
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: Active in early forcing but unable to sustain once YATES counterattacked, indicating tactical aggression without finish
rider_signals.FORMOLO Davide: neutral: Strong finish noted but unable to match YATES acceleration, confirming mid-tier classification threat
rider_signals.MASNADA Fausto: positive: Worked meaningfully in three-man break on behalf of Androni despite stage profile not suiting team objectives
